For all integers n > 1, if n is an odd prime then sigma(n) = n + 1.
For all integers n > 1, sigma(n) - n >= 1, with equality iff n is prime.
For all composite n, sigma(n) >= 2n.
For all composite n, sigma(n) - n >= n.
The number 2 is the unique even prime.
For all odd primes p, sigma(p) = p + 1 and p + 1 is even.

claim: For all odd prime p, sigma(p) is even.

For all n > 1, sigma(n) > n.
